title: quercus × helvetica
intro: Quercus × helvetica is a hybrid species of oak tree, resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in relation to its tannins and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus × helvetica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus × helvetica often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
